(created)   README.md (created)   analysis_options.yaml (created)   .idea/runConfigurations/main_dart.xml (created)   .idea/libraries/Dart_SDK.xml (created)   .idea/libraries/KotlinJavaRuntime.xml (created)   .idea/modules.xml (created)   .idea/workspace.xml (created)   test/widget_test.dart (created) Resolving dependencies... Downloading packages... Got dependencies. Wrote 37 files. All done! You can find general documentation for Flutter at: https://docs.flutter.dev/ Detailed API documentation is available at: https://api.flutter.dev/ If you prefer video documentation, consider: https://www.youtube.com/c/flutterdev In order to run your application, type:   $ flutter run Your application code is in ./lib/main.dart. [Flutter-UI] 21:24:06

✅ Excellent! The flutter create --platforms=macos . command worked perfectly. You now have a proper macOS desktop project with the macos/ folder and all necessary files.

However, because the command recreated some files (including lib/main.dart, analysis_options.yaml, etc.), it likely overwrote the custom files we had started building earlier. That’s why we need to carefully put everything back in place now.

Step-by-Step: Get a Clean, Running HomeScreen

Please do this in order:

  1. Run these two commands first (to make sure everything is fresh):
Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
  1. Replace your files with the clean versions below.

1. lib/main.dart

Dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'app.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const BibleJournalApp());
}

2. lib/app.dart

Dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'core/theme/app_theme.dart';
import 'features/home/presentation/home_screen.dart';

class BibleJournalApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const BibleJournalApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'BibleJournal',
      theme: AppTheme.lightTheme,
      home: const HomeScreen(),
      debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false,
    );
  }
}

3. Create folder structure if missing

Make sure these folders exist (create them if they don’t):

  • lib/core/theme/
  • lib/core/widgets/
  • lib/features/home/presentation/

4. lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart

Dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class AppTheme {
  static ThemeData get lightTheme {
    return ThemeData(
      useMaterial3: true,
      colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(
        seedColor: const Color(0xFF5C6B4A),        // Warm moss green
        brightness: Brightness.light,
        primary: const Color(0xFF5C6B4A),
        secondary: const Color(0xFF9C7A5B),        // Warm oak wood
        surface: const Color(0xFFF9F6F0),
        background: const Color(0xFFFBF9F4),
      ),
      scaffoldBackgroundColor: const Color(0xFFFBF9F4),
      cardTheme: CardTheme(
        elevation: 3,
        shadowColor: Colors.black.withOpacity(0.08),
        shape: RoundedRectangleBorder(borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(20)),
      ),
      textTheme: const TextTheme(
        headlineLarge: TextStyle(
          fontSize: 40,
          fontWeight: FontWeight.w300,
          letterSpacing: -0.8,
          color: Color(0xFF2F3A2A),
        ),
        headlineMedium: TextStyle(fontSize: 26, fontWeight: FontWeight.w500),
        bodyLarge: TextStyle(fontSize: 17.5, height: 1.65),
      ),
      elevatedButtonTheme: ElevatedButtonThemeData(
        style: ElevatedButton.styleFrom(
          backgroundColor: const Color(0xFF5C6B4A),
          foregroundColor: Colors.white,
          padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 36, vertical: 18),
          shape: RoundedRectangleBorder(borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(14)),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

5. lib/core/widgets/desk_header.dart

Dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class DeskHeader extends StatelessWidget {
  const DeskHeader({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Row(
      children: [
        Container(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(14),
          decoration: BoxDecoration(
            color: const Color(0xFF5C6B4A).withOpacity(0.08),
            borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(16),
          ),
          child: const Icon(Icons.edit_note, size: 52, color: Color(0xFF5C6B4A)),
        ),
        const SizedBox(width: 24),
        const Column(
          crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
          children: [
            Text(
              'BibleJournal',
              style: TextStyle(
                fontSize: 28,
                fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
                letterSpacing: 0.6,
              ),
            ),
            Text(
              'V2 • Your daily desk at the edge of the Park',
              style: TextStyle(fontSize: 14, color: Colors.grey),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ],
    );
  }
}

6. lib/features/home/presentation/home_screen.dart

Dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:intl/intl.dart';
import '../../../core/widgets/desk_header.dart';

class HomeScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  const HomeScreen({super.key});

  String _getGreeting() {
    final hour = DateTime.now().hour;
    if (hour < 12) return 'Good morning';
    if (hour < 17) return 'Good afternoon';
    return 'Good evening';
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    final today = DateTime.now();
    final formattedDate = DateFormat('EEEE, MMMM d, y').format(today);

    return Scaffold(
      body: SafeArea(
        child: SingleChildScrollView(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 48, vertical: 40),
          child: Column(
            crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
            children: [
              const DeskHeader(),
              const SizedBox(height: 52),

              Text(
                '${_getGreeting()}, Pete.',
                style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headlineLarge,
              ),
              const SizedBox(height: 8),
              Text(
                'Welcome to your study desk.\nThe Park is open and waiting.',
                style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.bodyLarge?.copyWith(
                      color: Colors.grey[700],
                    ),
              ),

              const SizedBox(height: 64),

              // Today’s M’Cheyne Reading Card
              Card(
                child: Padding(
                  padding: const EdgeInsets.all(40),
                  child: Column(
                    crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
                    children: [
                      Row(
                        children: [
                          const Icon(Icons.bookmark_border, size: 36, color: Color(0xFF5C6B4A)),
                          const SizedBox(width: 20),
                          Text(
                            'Today’s M’Cheyne Reading',
                            style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headlineMedium,
                          ),
                        ],
                      ),
                      const SizedBox(height: 6),
                      Text(
                        formattedDate,
                        style: const TextStyle(fontSize: 17, color: Colors.grey),
                      ),

                      const SizedBox(height: 32),

                      const _ReadingItem(label: 'Morning', reading: 'Genesis 1 • Psalm 1'),
                      const _ReadingItem(label: 'Evening', reading: 'Proverbs 1 • Matthew 1'),

                      const SizedBox(height: 40),
                      Center(
                        child: ElevatedButton.icon(
                          icon: const Icon(Icons.arrow_forward),
                          label: const Text('Open Today’s Readings'),
                          onPressed: () {
                            ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).showSnackBar(
                              const SnackBar(content: Text('Opening today’s readings... (coming soon)')),
                            );
                          },
                        ),
                      ),
                    ],
                  ),
                ),
              ),

              const SizedBox(height: 72),

              // Quick Journal Teaser
              Text(
                'Your Journal',
                style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headlineMedium,
              ),
              const SizedBox(height: 20),
              Card(
                child: Padding(
                  padding: const EdgeInsets.all(28),
                  child: Column(
                    crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
                    children: [
                      const Text(
                        'What stood out to you today?',
                        style: TextStyle(fontSize: 18, fontWeight: FontWeight.w500),
                      ),
                      const SizedBox(height: 12),
                      TextField(
                        decoration: InputDecoration(
                          hintText: 'Write a short reflection...',
                          border: OutlineInputBorder(
                            borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(12),
                            borderSide: BorderSide(color: Colors.grey.shade300),
                          ),
                          filled: true,
                          fillColor: const Color(0xFFFBF9F4),
                        ),
                        maxLines: 3,
                      ),
                      const SizedBox(height: 16),
                      Align(
                        alignment: Alignment.centerRight,
                        child: TextButton.icon(
                          onPressed: () {},
                          icon: const Icon(Icons.save_alt),
                          label: const Text('Save Entry'),
                        ),
                      ),
                    ],
                  ),
                ),
              ),

              const SizedBox(height: 80),

              Center(
                child: Opacity(
                  opacity: 0.75,
                  child: Text(
                    'This is your quiet space.\nThe Scriptures themselves are the real Park.',
                    textAlign: TextAlign.center,
                    style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.bodyMedium?.copyWith(
                          fontStyle: FontStyle.italic,
                        ),
                  ),
                ),
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class _ReadingItem extends StatelessWidget {
  final String label;
  final String reading;

  const _ReadingItem({required this.label, required this.reading});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Padding(
      padding: const EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 16),
      child: Row(
        crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
        children: [
          SizedBox(
            width: 90,
            child: Text(
              label,
              style: const TextStyle(fontWeight: FontWeight.w600, fontSize: 16),
            ),
          ),
          Expanded(
            child: Text(
              reading,
              style: const TextStyle(fontSize: 17.5),
            ),
          ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}

Final Commands After Pasting All Files

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

Now run those commands and tell me what happens.

If the app launches and you see the warm study desk UI with “Good morning/afternoon/evening, Pete.”, we’re in great shape and can start polishing or move to the next screen.

If you get any error, just paste it here. Let’s get this running!

790ms

Flutter-UI] flutter run -d macos 21:28:38 Launching lib/main.dart on macOS in debug mode... ProcessException: Process exited abnormally with exit code 72: xcrun: error: unable to find utility "xcodebuild", not a developer tool or in PATH   Command: /usr/bin/arch -arm64e xcrun xcodebuild -list -project Runner.xcodeproj [Flutter-UI] 21:28:48   and problems: 5

✅ Got it, Pete. This is a classic Xcode Command Line Tools setup issue on macOS.

The error xcrun: error: unable to find utility "xcodebuild" means Flutter can’t find the Xcode build tools, even though you added macOS support. This is very common after installing or updating Xcode (or when the project is first created on a new machine).

Fix It in Two Easy Steps

Step 1: Run this command in your terminal (inside the Flutter-UI folder)

Bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
  • You may be asked for your Mac password.
  • This tells macOS where the Xcode developer tools are located.

Step 2: Accept the Xcode license (important)

Bash
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

After running both commands, try building again:

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

Alternative (Graphical Way – if you prefer)

  1. Open Xcode (if it's not installed yet, download it from the Mac App Store — it's free but large ~10-12 GB).
  2. Once Xcode opens, go to Xcode → Settings → Locations.
  3. In the Command Line Tools dropdown, select the version of Xcode you have installed.
  4. Close Xcode and try flutter run -d macos again.

After It Works

The 5 remaining problems should also clear up once the build succeeds (they are likely related to the analyzer waiting for the project to be properly configured).

When the app launches, you should see the warm BibleJournal study desk UI with:

  • The DeskHeader
  • Dynamic greeting (“Good morning/afternoon/evening, Pete.”)
  • Today’s M’Cheyne card
  • Journal teaser

Reply with the result after running the commands above:

  • Did flutter run -d macos succeed?
  • Any new error messages?
  • Did the app window open?

If Xcode itself is not installed yet, you’ll need to install it from the App Store first (it takes time to download). Let me know if that’s the case and I’ll guide you on the minimal setup needed for macOS desktop only.

Once the app is running visually, we can immediately start refining the look (subtle background, better spacing, etc.) or move to the next screen (Reading View).

You’re almost there — this is the last common macOS setup hurdle!

1.1s
15 sources

[Flutter-UI] sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer 21:28:48 Password: xcode-select: error: invalid developer directory '/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer' [Flutter-UI] 21:30:41

✅ Got it — the error is clear now.

The command failed because Xcode is not installed (or not installed in the expected location /Applications/Xcode.app).

Flutter needs the Xcode Command Line Tools (specifically xcodebuild) to build macOS desktop apps. Since the full Xcode app is missing, the path /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer doesn't exist.

Best and Simplest Solution for You Right Now

Since you're only building for macOS desktop (not iOS), you have two options. I recommend the lighter option first:

Option 1: Install Only Command Line Tools (Recommended — smaller & faster)

Run this command in your terminal:

Bash
xcode-select --install
  • A popup window will appear saying "The xcode-select command requires the command line developer tools. Would you like to install the tools now?"
  • Click Install and wait for it to download and finish (usually 5–10 minutes, depending on your internet).
  • Once it completes, run:
Bash
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

Then try building your app again:

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

Option 2: Install the Full Xcode (if you want everything, including future iOS support)

  1. Open the Mac App Store.
  2. Search for Xcode and install it (it's free, but ~10–12 GB — it will take a while to download).
  3. Once installed and opened for the first time, run these commands:
Bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

Then:

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

Quick Check After Installation

After installing (either option), run this to verify:

Bash
xcode-select -p

It should show a valid path (either /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools or /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer).

Please try Option 1 first (xcode-select --install) as it's the quickest for macOS desktop development.

Once the installation finishes and flutter run -d macos succeeds, the app should launch with the warm study desk UI we built (dynamic greeting for Pete, Today’s M’Cheyne card, etc.).

Let me know what happens after you run xcode-select --install — paste the result or any new message. We'll get the UI showing right after that.

You're very close now!

1.1s
25 sources

[Flutter-UI] xcode-select --install 21:30:41 xcode-select: note: Command line tools are already installed. Use "Software Update" in System Settings or the softwareupdate command line interface to install updates

✅ Thanks for the update.

Even though xcode-select --install says the Command Line Tools are already installed, Flutter for macOS desktop almost always requires the full Xcode app to be installed (not just the lightweight Command Line Tools). This is a known behavior in recent Flutter versions.

What to do now (Recommended Path)

Install the full Xcode — it's the most reliable way to fix this.

  1. Open the Mac App Store on your Mac.
  2. Search for "Xcode" (made by Apple).
  3. Click Get / Install (it's free, but the download is large — around 10–12 GB, so it may take 20–60+ minutes depending on your internet speed).

Once the download and installation finish:

  • Open Xcode at least once (it will do some initial setup).
  • Then run these commands in your terminal (inside the Flutter-UI folder):
Bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
sudo xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch

After that, try building again:

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

Alternative (Faster but sometimes less stable)

If you don't want to download the full Xcode right now, try forcing the Command Line Tools path:

Bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

But in most cases (especially with Flutter 3.41+), the full Xcode is needed for macOS desktop builds.

While Xcode is downloading

You can run flutter doctor -v again later to confirm the Xcode section turns green.

Once the app successfully launches, you should see the BibleJournal study desk UI we prepared (with the dynamic greeting for you, the Today’s M’Cheyne card, etc.).

Let me know when Xcode finishes installing (or if you try the alternative command first) and what the new output is from flutter run -d macos. We'll get past this setup step and start making the UI even nicer.

Hang in there — this is the final common hurdle for macOS desktop in Flutter.

1.1s
11 sources

While it's downloading again, should I close my current instance (the full IDE version)?

Yes — good question.

While Xcode is downloading and installing from the Mac App Store, here’s the cleanest approach:

Recommended Right Now:

Close your current VS Code / Cursor window (the Flutter-UI workspace).

Why?

  • The Flutter extension sometimes gets confused when Xcode is being installed or updated in the background.
  • Closing VS Code avoids any lock on files or cached analyzer issues.

What to do while waiting for Xcode:

  1. Close the current VS Code window completely (Cmd + Q).
  2. You can leave the terminal open if you want, but it’s not necessary.
  3. Just wait for the Xcode download + install to finish (it will show progress in the App Store).

After Xcode finishes installing:

Do these steps in order:

  1. Open Xcode once (let it complete its first-launch setup — it may take a few extra minutes).
  2. Quit Xcode after it opens.
  3. Re-open your BibleJournal V2 Flutter-Dart UI.code-workspace.
  4. In the terminal inside the Flutter-UI folder, run:
Bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

This sequence usually resolves the xcodebuild error cleanly.

While you wait for the download:

Would you like me to prepare the next improvements we can make once the app is running?

For example, I can give you an updated version of the HomeScreen with:

  • A subtle warm wooden desk / parchment background
  • Better visual spacing and hierarchy
  • A small “Dash” style navigation hint at the bottom

Or we can start planning the Reading View screen (the calm page you land on when tapping “Open Today’s Readings”).

Just say “ready” when Xcode is done and the app launches, or tell me what you’d like to work on while waiting.

You’re doing great — this big download is the last heavy lifting for the macOS setup. Once it’s finished, we’ll have a nice running app and can focus purely on the beautiful, calm UI.

1s

Should I include the IOS version?

No, you should NOT include the iOS version right now.

Here's the clear recommendation for your BibleJournal V2 project:

Keep it simple — only macOS desktop for now

Reasons:

  • Your goal is a calm desktop experience (starting with macOS) that feels like a welcoming study desk / front porch.
  • Adding iOS now would bring extra complexity (different UI patterns for mobile, more configuration, larger project size, and stricter Apple signing requirements later).
  • The current flutter run -d macos is exactly what we want — a native macOS window with generous spacing, wide layout, and a peaceful desktop feel.
  • We can always add iOS (or Android/web) later with one command when you're ready.

What to do while Xcode finishes downloading

Since you're already downloading the full Xcode (good choice), let it complete.

After it finishes installing and you've run the setup commands I gave earlier (sudo xcode-select -s ..., sudo xcodebuild -license accept, etc.), run:

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

This should finally launch your app on macOS without the xcodebuild error.

If you want to be extra clean (optional)

Once the app is running successfully, you can remove iOS support if it was accidentally added during the flutter create step:

Bash
flutter create --platforms=macos .

(This keeps only macOS and prevents unnecessary iOS folders/files from cluttering things.)

My suggestion for the project scope right now:

Focus on:

  • Beautiful, spacious macOS desktop UI
  • Calm, reverent “study desk” metaphor
  • M’Cheyne daily reading flow
  • Journaling features

We’ll make it feel excellent on desktop first. Later, if you want a mobile companion app, we can add iOS/Android support and adapt the UI (more compact, touch-friendly) using Flutter’s responsive tools.

While Xcode downloads, would you like me to prepare:

  • An improved version of the HomeScreen with a subtle warm wooden/parchment background?
  • Or start designing the Reading View screen (the calm page for viewing the actual daily passages with space for notes)?

Just say the word once Xcode is done and the app launches successfully. We'll pick up right there and make it look really inviting.

Hang tight — you're almost past the setup phase!

1s
20 sources

How about their new predictive code completion model?

Yes, I think you're referring to the newer AI-powered predictive code completion features available in 2026 — especially the advanced inline suggestions, multi-line predictions, and "predictive editing" capabilities that go beyond traditional IntelliSense.

Here's a clear breakdown tailored to your BibleJournal V2 Flutter project in VS Code:

Current State of Predictive Code Completion (2026)

  • GitHub Copilot (inside VS Code) Still the most popular choice for Flutter/Dart developers who want to stay in standard VS Code. It now supports model selection (you can switch between different underlying models for completions). Inline ghost text suggestions are faster and smarter for Dart/Flutter patterns (widgets, Riverpod providers, theme extensions, etc.). Good for single-line or small-block completions while typing.
  • Cursor (the standalone AI-first editor based on VS Code) This is what many people mean by "their new predictive code completion model." Cursor's Cursor Tab (or predictive editing) is significantly more aggressive and powerful — it doesn't just finish the current line; it can predict and suggest multi-step edits across your file or even multiple files. Many Flutter devs find it faster for building UI screens, refactoring widget trees, or wiring up state management.
  • Other strong options:
    • Continue.dev extension in VS Code → free/local models (Ollama + strong coder models like Qwen 2.5 Coder).
    • Claude-based tools (Claude Code / Sonnet models) — often praised for understanding Flutter widget patterns and clean Dart code very well.

My Recommendation for You Right Now

Since you're building a calm, clean Flutter UI project and we're iterating together with clear, teachable code:

  1. Stick with VS Code + GitHub Copilot for the moment (easiest and least disruptive). Install the official GitHub Copilot extension if you haven't already. You can then go to the Copilot status bar and change the completions model if multiple options appear.
  2. Try Cursor if you want the "wow" predictive experience. It's excellent for Flutter work (especially UI-heavy screens like our study desk + reading views). Many people switch to Cursor when they want the AI to anticipate larger chunks of Flutter code (e.g., building a whole Card with proper padding, theming, and responsiveness).

Would you like me to:

  • Give you the exact setup steps for GitHub Copilot in your current VS Code workspace (including how to pick a better model for Dart/Flutter)?
  • Or walk you through installing and switching to Cursor (it imports your VS Code settings easily)?
  • Or show you a good free/local setup using Continue.dev so you don't need a paid subscription?

Also, once your app is finally running (after Xcode finishes), the AI completion will be much more useful because it can see the full context of our HomeScreen, AppTheme, widgets, etc.

Just tell me which direction you'd like to go, and I'll give you precise steps.

In the meantime, how is the Xcode download progressing? Once it's done and the app launches, we can jump straight back into refining the warm study-desk UI with better predictive help from the AI tools.

969ms
25 sources

Ready to open existing project.

✅ Perfect! You're ready to open the existing BibleJournal V2 Flutter-UI project cleanly.

How to Open It Properly in VS Code

  1. Close any current VS Code windows (Cmd + Q) to avoid cached issues.

  2. Re-open using the workspace file (this is the cleanest way):

    • In Finder, go to: /Volumes/OWC_Envoy_Ultra/Projects/BibleJournal-V2/Flutter-UI
    • Double-click the file: BibleJournal V2 Flutter-Dart UI.code-workspace (If you don’t see the .code-workspace file, create/open it via File → Open Workspace from File...)

    This should load the project with the correct name “Flutter UI” in the Explorer.

  3. Once open, run these commands in the integrated terminal (bottom panel):

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get
  1. Restart the Dart analyzer:
    • Press Cmd + Shift + P
    • Type and select Dart: Restart Analysis Server

After Xcode Finishes Installing

When the full Xcode download completes from the App Store:

  • Open Xcode once (let it finish its initial setup).
  • Then run these commands in the terminal:
Bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
flutter clean
flutter pub get
flutter run -d macos

This should finally launch the app on macOS desktop.

Quick Tip While Waiting for Xcode

Once the app runs successfully and you see the study desk UI, the 5 remaining problems should disappear (they're mostly analyzer noise from the incomplete Xcode setup).

Would you like me to give you a refreshed, improved version of the HomeScreen with:

  • A subtle warm wooden desk / parchment background (using a gradient + optional image)
  • Even better spacing and visual hierarchy for desktop
  • A small sidebar navigation teaser (the start of our “Dash” UI)

Or shall we wait until the app is actually running, then polish it together?

Just say “Xcode done” or paste the output when you try flutter run -d macos again, and we'll get the beautiful, calm BibleJournal lobby/study desk on screen immediately.

You're doing great — setup is almost complete!

1.1s
10 sources

The workspace is empty: { "folders": [ { "path": "." } ] }

✅ Got it — the workspace file is basically empty, which is why everything looks blank.

This happened because when we recreated the project with flutter create --platforms=macos ., it didn't automatically update your custom workspace file.

Fix: Update Your Workspace File (Simple)

  1. In your Flutter-UI folder, find or create the file named: BibleJournal V2 Flutter-Dart UI.code-workspace
  2. Replace its entire content with this:
JSON
{
  "folders": [
    {
      "name": "BibleJournal V2 Flutter UI",
      "path": "."
    }
  ],
  "settings": {
    "editor.formatOnSave": true,
    "dart.previewUpdateImportsOnRename": true,
    "files.exclude": {
      "**/.dart_tool": true,
      "**/.pub-cache": true,
      "**/build": true
    }
  }
}
  1. Save the file.
  2. Close and reopen the workspace:
    • Close current VS Code window (Cmd + Q)
    • Double-click the BibleJournal V2 Flutter-Dart UI.code-workspace file again to reopen it.

Now the Explorer should show your full project structure (lib/, macos/, pubspec.yaml, etc.) instead of being empty.

Next Steps After Reopening

Once the workspace loads properly with all folders visible:

Run in the terminal:

Bash
flutter clean
flutter pub get

Then restart the analyzer:

  • Cmd + Shift + P → Dart: Restart Analysis Server

After that, we can proceed with launching the app (once Xcode is ready).

Would you like me to also give you a cleaner, updated set of files (main.dart, app.dart, HomeScreen with subtle background) that you can paste once the workspace looks normal again?

Just confirm when the workspace is no longer empty and all your lib/ folders are visible, then we'll get the warm study desk UI running.

1.1s

Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information

Cookies are data files that can hold and store small amounts of information about you, your preferences, or your devices. Some cookies are required to make the website function, others are optional and used to improve and personalize your experience. You can choose to block optional cookies on our website below, just be aware it may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information

Manage Consent Preferences

Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

Performance Cookies

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

Sell, Share, or Use Your Data for Targeted Advertising

We may collect personal data from and about you through this site for “selling,” “sharing,” or “targeted advertising,” as defined by applicable privacy laws. To opt out, move the toggle to the left and go to the bottom of this pop-up and click “Confirm My Choices.”
